title: Information Backup ‚Äî Immutable Backup Bucket Policy Change Attempts

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.13 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-30d (eventName="PutBucketObjectLockConfiguration" OR eventName="DeleteObjectLockConfiguration" OR eventName="PutLifecycleConfiguration")
| stats count by userIdentity.arn, eventName
| sort - count
description: Alerts on API calls that would weaken object-lock or immutability on backup storage.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes aws and sourcetypes aws:cloudtrail. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Scope backup accounts; (2) SOC sev-1 playbook; (3) map to backup integrity SoA control. Indexes required: `index=aws`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: AWS CloudTrail.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:cloudtrail`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Scope backup accounts; (2) SOC sev-1 playbook; (3) map to backup integrity SoA control.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-30d (eventName="PutBucketObjectLockConfiguration" OR eventName="DeleteObjectLockConfiguration" OR eventName="PutLifecycleConfiguration")
| stats count by userIdentity.arn, eventName
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Information Backup ‚Äî Immutable Backup Bucket Policy Change Attempts (A.8.13)** ‚Äî Alerts on API calls that would weaken object-lock or immutability on backup storage.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AWS CloudTrail. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by userIdentity.arn, eventName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Information Backup ‚Äî Immutable Backup Bucket Policy Change Attempts (A.8.13)** ‚Äî Alerts on API calls that would weaken object-lock or immutability on backup storage.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AWS CloudTrail. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline
grandmaExplanation: We watch for alerts on api calls that would weaken object-lock or immutability on backup storage. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
